display_name: CAPTURE A/B (2026-07-13, scratchpad/_sol_capture_exit_0713.md). Entry byte-identical to badday_young_rt_paper -> ONLY the EXIT ladder differs. PAPER, own singleton pool (full token stream, paired-by-token grading), live_probe=false, NEVER routes live. ADDITIVE to the deployed exit-A/B family (control/minhold/barbell/heatrunner/minhold_heat): targets the CAPTURE leak measured on the 07-11..13 tape (277 young-lane positions) = the 25% post-TP1 REMAINDER round-tripping to -9..-13 on small winners ([6,12) capture-eff 0.41, leak 5.3pp) and a few monster reversals ([30+] leak 8.9pp). Fix = TIGHT peak-tracked runner instead of a fixed +12 full-out: bank 0.60 @ TP1 +6 (the reliable scalp, unchanged), small 0.10 @ +12, keep a 0.30 house-money moonbag with a BREAKEVEN floor (0%, can never round-trip to a loss) and a TIGHT 3pp peak-trail (= the winners' ~2.6% median give-back; trail_reprice_shadow recovered +2.7pp median on this tape). This is the SAFE-direction bet vs a fat moonbag (moonbag_ab caught the most tail, 16.7% reached +30, yet had the WORST median -7.7 -> a wide bag BLEEDS). Combined with the min-hold 120s floor (the ONLY proven ex-top-2 mover: replay ex2 -5.69->+3.53 GREEN 4/4, cat 1.0%). Replay combined: ex2 +3.53 GREEN 4/4 (min-half +1.43), mean +3.37; the moonbag/trail leg is an MFE-truncated LOWER bound -> forward-confirm the capture add. Grade vs exit_control at n>=30 closes, ex-top-2 AND captured-pp, OOS 3/4 halves, cat<=1/20.
